checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked the requested interaction against the existing timeline UI in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,774-803` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:107-201`. The repo’s established pattern for these affordances is a portal context menu, but the user request explicitly says clicking the badge should open a panel; the plan changes that to a menu/mini-menu, which is a product-level divergence that needs an explicit acceptance rather than being treated as equivalent.

Checked how shot groups are defined in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:29-33,73-75` and in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.test.ts:101-110`. The current repo only materializes shot groups when there are at least two matching clips, so the plan’s assumption that single-clip shots will not show the final-video badge is consistent with current code, but it still leaves one of the plan’s own open questions unresolved and narrows the feature unless that scope choice is confirmed.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked the `shot_final_videos` view definition in `supabase/migrations/20260130230000_critical_fix_view_security.sql:53-71` and the generated types in `src/integrations/supabase/types.ts:1545-1580`. The view’s `id` column is the underlying `generations.id`, so using `finalVideo.id` as the timeline asset `generationId` is technically consistent with the repository’s schema.

Checked the proposed asset-registration call against the actual drag-drop contract in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:48-55` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:66-85`. The plan says to call `registerGenerationAsset({ generationId: finalVideo.id, imageUrl: finalVideo.location, mimeType: 'video/mp4' })`, but `mimeType` is not a supported field on `GenerationDropData`, and `registerGenerationAsset()` only looks at `metadata.content_type`, `variantType`, or the URL suffix; as written, this call can misclassify the asset unless the location reliably ends with a video extension.

Checked `useShotGroups()` in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:79-130` and the planned `TimelineCanvas` callback shape that only passes `shotId`. The repository can emit multiple distinct groups for the same shot across rows or separated runs on one row, so a switch action keyed only by `shotId` is ambiguous and can replace the wrong clip set; the callback needs the clicked group identity (`clipIds`, `rowId`, or a group key), not just the shot id.

Checked the edit pipeline in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:74-95,220-236` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:193-216`. A single atomic replace edit is feasible with one `applyEdit({ type: 'rows', ... })` call because `metaUpdates` and `metaDeletes` are both supported on the existing rows mutation path.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Checked the existing video-drop paths in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55,386-412` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAssetManagement.ts:198-252`. The broader repository pattern for video assets is not just “register then insert”; multi-generation video drops also patch `registry.duration` using `getDroppedGenerationDuration()`, while `useShotFinalVideos()` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useShotFinalVideos.ts:19-37` currently fetches no duration metadata. That means final-video replacement is broader than badge wiring alone if the new clip is expected to behave like other video assets in timing UI and resize logic.

Checked shot-group action plumbing in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.tsx:343-364,757-803`. The canvas already has one shot-group menu state and portal renderer for group actions, so introducing a second parallel menu state for final-video actions would duplicate the same concern in the same component; extending the existing menu model with richer group payload looks more aligned with the current structure.

Checked the commit path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts:210-216` and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts:217-255`. The repository already has the supporting infrastructure to rebuild rows, meta, and serialized config from one rows edit, so the replacement feature does not need any new persistence layer beyond the existing editor mutation flow.

checks.question: Is there convincing verification for the change?

findings.detail: Checked the current automated coverage in `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx:133-260` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts:1-114`. Existing tests only cover resize behavior and a couple of pure helpers; they do not exercise shot-group overlays, shot-group menus, or any final-video replacement path, so the plan’s proposed verification is too weak for the main UI and mutation risks.

Checked for any existing tests around `useShotFinalVideos` or `shot_final_videos` in the editor paths and found none. A new `useFinalVideoAvailable` hook test would verify filtering and dismiss state, but it would still miss the highest-risk behavior: removing a specific group’s clips, inserting a single video clip, registering the asset with the right media type, and keeping the operation as one undoable edit.

Checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.test.ts:47-123` and the existing `applyEdit` assertion style there. The repo already has a straightforward pattern for unit-testing editor mutations by asserting the exact `applyEdit` payload, so the plan should add a targeted test for the switch-to-final-video edit shape rather than relying mostly on manual smoke coverage.

flags.concern: Shot-group replacement targeting: Recurring debt: the plan keys the switch action by `shotId`, but `useShotGroups()` can produce multiple distinct groups for the same shot, so the replacement target is ambiguous and can hit the wrong row or clip set.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useShotGroups.ts:79-130` can emit multiple groups per shot, while the planned callbacks only pass `shotId` from `TimelineCanvas`.

flags.concern: Asset registration contract: the proposed `registerGenerationAsset()` call uses an unsupported `mimeType` field, so final videos may not be registered as video assets unless the plan passes `variantType: 'video'` or `metadata.content_type`.
flags.evidence: `GenerationDropData` in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:48-55` has no `mimeType`, and `useAssetManagement.ts:71-85` infers media type only from `metadata.content_type`, `variantType`, or the URL.

flags.concern: Verification coverage: the planned checks do not exercise the new shot-group badge/menu wiring or the atomic replace mutation, so the main risk surface remains untested.
flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` currently covers resize behavior only, and `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.test.ts` contains only helper tests.
